meta.title: Holodeck Audit
meta.subject: What a third-party scene plugin can carry — without forking @bjelser/core

scenes.title: Holodeck Audit
scenes.tagline: A third-party scene type, registered through the same protocol @bjelser/core uses
scenes.footnote: Loaded from @example/docent-scifi  ·  preset: scifi-noir  ·  zero edits to @bjelser/core

beats.narration: Open the panel. This film is the architectural proof — a custom scene type and a custom preset rendered by the same engine that ships the docent default twenty-nine.
If the next minute renders without forking the core package, the framework slash implementation split is real.

scenes.title: Custom scene type
scenes.subtitle: holodeckPlugin lives in @example/docent-scifi — yet engine.scenes.get('holodeck') returns it

beats.narration: This scene is not in the docent core library. It is a holodeck — invented by a third-party package, registered through the same plugin protocol the default twenty-nine use.
The engine sniffed its kind, routed it to the scene registry, and the cascade dispatched its component without knowing where it came from.

nodes.label: Plugin protocol
nodes.sub: closed — breaking it is a major bump

nodes.label: Scene library
nodes.sub: open — anyone can register a sceneType

beats.narration: The bet is structural. The plugin protocol is closed — break it and every third-party plugin breaks. The scene library itself is open — anyone can register a new cognitive move.
And what enforces quality across the open library is not curation. It is the depthcheck and judge contract every scene must honor — built-in or third-party, same bar.

scenes.heading: The platform bet, made concrete
scenes.statement: The 29 reference scenes are an opinionated default. The scene library is open. The contract is the wall.

beats.narration: Here is the line to leave with. Twenty-nine reference scenes are the opinionated default — they are not the closed universe.
The library extends by N — by however many third-party scenes the protocol can carry — and the contract every scene honors is what holds the discipline.

scenes.title: Acceptance: GREEN
scenes.subtitle: If this frame renders, the rip-and-replace has cleared its hardest test

beats.narration: This second holodeck panel is the same plugin, rendered again. The engine resolved it from the same registry, painted it with the same custom preset, and threaded it into the timeline.

scenes.heading: Framework, implementation, third-party — three packages, one protocol
points: @bjelser/kit owns the protocol and the registry — zero opinions.
@bjelser/core registers the 29 default scenes through that same public API.

beats.narration: Three packages, one protocol. The kit owns the rules. The core ships the reference twenty-nine. A third party adds the thirtieth — through the same door, never around it.
The architecture is done when this film renders. It just rendered.
